proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than those who do not.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 5.408 on palbociclib (n=17428) vs 4.310 off (n=32572); difference +1.098, Welch t-test p<1e-300.

analyses.code: Welch t-test on pfs_months by treatment_tamoxifen
analyses.result_summary: Mean 4.723 on tamoxifen vs 4.680 off; difference +0.043 months, p=0.077. Not significant.

analyses.code: Welch t-test on pfs_months by treatment_trastuzumab
analyses.result_summary: Mean 4.645 on trastuzumab vs 4.705 off; difference -0.060 months, p=0.029 (direction opposite to hypothesis).

analyses.code: Welch t-test on pfs_months by treatment_olaparib
analyses.result_summary: Mean 4.660 on olaparib vs 4.696 off; difference -0.036 months, p=0.33. Not significant.

analyses.code: Welch t-test on pfs_months by treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan
analyses.result_summary: Mean 4.686 on sacituzumab govitecan vs 4.694 off; difference -0.007 months, p=0.85. Not significant.

analyses.code: Welch t-test on pfs_months by treatment_pembrolizumab
analyses.result_summary: Mean 4.630 on pembrolizumab vs 4.704 off; difference -0.074 months, p=0.018 (direction opposite to hypothesis).

proposed_hypotheses.text: er_positive=1 patients have longer pfs_months than er_positive=0.
pr_positive=1 patients have longer pfs_months than pr_positive=0.
her2_positive=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than her2_positive=0.
her2_low=1 patients have different pfs_months than her2_low=0.
brca1_mutation=1 patients have different pfs_months than brca1_mutation=0.
brca2_mutation=1 patients have different pfs_months than brca2_mutation=0.
pik3ca_mutation=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than pik3ca_mutation=0.
stage_iv=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
has_brain_mets=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0.
node_positive=1 patients have different pfs_months than node_positive=0.
postmenopausal=1 patients have different pfs_months than postmenopausal=0.
sex_female=1 patients have different pfs_months than sex_female=0.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (monotone gradient).

analyses.result_summary: Mean 4.824 in PR+ vs 4.462 in PR-; difference +0.362, p=5.8e-59.
Mean 4.317 in HER2+ vs 4.776 in HER2-; difference -0.459, p=6.1e-70.
Mean 4.765 in HER2-low vs 4.650 otherwise; difference +0.114, p=1.0e-6.
Mean 4.669 in BRCA1+ (n=1277) vs 4.694 BRCA1- ; difference -0.024, p=0.72. Not significant.
Mean 4.569 in BRCA2+ (n=1222) vs 4.696 BRCA2- ; difference -0.127, p=0.081. Borderline.
Mean 4.328 in PIK3CA-mut (n=17365) vs 4.887 PIK3CA-wt; difference -0.559, p=6.1e-144.
Mean 3.617 in stage IV vs 5.153 non-stage IV; difference -1.536, p<1e-300.
Mean 3.750 with brain mets vs 4.800 without; difference -1.050, p=1.45e-178.
Mean 4.681 vs 4.705; difference -0.024, p=0.29. Not significant.
Mean 4.684 vs 4.707; difference -0.023, p=0.31. Not significant.
Mean 4.686 in females vs 4.699 in males; difference -0.013, p=0.56. Not significant. (Notable: cohort is only 44.7% female.)
Means 5.640 / 4.444 / 3.289 for ecog 0/1/2 (n=17592/24971/7437); strict monotone decrease, F=2861, p<1e-300. Approx -1.17 months per ECOG point.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher age_years correlates with pfs_months (direction unknown a priori).
Higher albumin_g_dl correlates with longer pfs_months.
Higher ki67_pct correlates with shorter pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo correlates with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l correlates with shorter pfs_months.
Inflammatory markers crp_mg_l and nlr correlate with shorter pfs_months.
Routine labs (hemoglobin, alk phos, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium, tumor_size_cm) have negligible association with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.7015, p<1e-300. PFS rises monotonically across age deciles (decile 0 mean PFS 1.70, decile 9 mean PFS 7.84). Older patients have substantially LONGER PFS in this cohort - opposite of the usual oncology direction.
r=+0.100, p=9e-112. Higher albumin -> longer PFS, as expected.
r=-0.091, p=3e-93. Higher proliferation -> shorter PFS, as expected.
r=-0.116, p=4e-148. More cachexia -> shorter PFS, as expected.
r=-0.016, p=2.5e-4. Tiny but significant negative correlation.

analyses.code: Pearson r for crp_mg_l and nlr
analyses.result_summary: crp_mg_l r=-0.003 (p=0.46); nlr r=+0.003 (p=0.54). Neither inflammatory marker has a meaningful univariate association with PFS.

analyses.code: Pearson r for hemoglobin, alk_phos, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium, tumor_size_cm
analyses.result_summary: All |r| < 0.014. tumor_size_cm r=+0.0004 (p=0.93). Most are statistically null at alpha=0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model of pfs_months on all features and treatments, treatment_palbociclib retains a substantial positive coefficient while tamoxifen, trastuzumab, olaparib, sacituzumab govitecan, and pembrolizumab do not.

analyses.result_summary: R^2=0.792. treatment_palbociclib coef +1.101 (p<1e-300). All other treatments null after adjustment: tamoxifen -0.008 (p=0.48), trastuzumab -0.009 (p=0.47), olaparib -0.026 (p=0.12), sacituzumab govitecan +0.033 (p=0.049, marginal), pembrolizumab -0.020 (p=0.17). Strongest non-treatment predictors: ecog_ps -1.17, stage_iv -1.55, has_brain_mets -0.98, age_years +0.176/yr, albumin +0.48/g/dL, weight_loss -0.077/%, ki67 -0.019/%, er_positive +0.53, her2_positive -0.47, pik3ca_mutation -0.58.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months more in er_positive=1 patients than in er_positive=0 patients (positive interaction).
treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months differs by postmenopausal status.
treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is reduced in her2_positive=1 patients (negative interaction).
treatment_tamoxifen increases pfs_months more in er_positive=1 patients (positive interaction).
treatment_trastuzumab increases pfs_months more in her2_positive=1 patients (positive interaction).
treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months more in brca1_mutation=1 patients (positive interaction).
treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months more in brca2_mutation=1 patients (positive interaction).
treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan increases pfs_months more in her2_low=1 patients (positive interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months differs by pik3ca_mutation status.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient +1.559 (p<1e-300). Palbociclib effect ~+0.012 in ER- vs ~+1.571 in ER+. Massive ER-driven heterogeneity.
Interaction coefficient -0.026, p=0.24. No meaningful modification by menopausal status.
Interaction coefficient -1.338 (p<1e-300). Palbociclib effect +1.346 in HER2- vs +0.008 in HER2+. HER2+ abolishes palbociclib benefit.
Interaction +0.007 (p=0.78). No tamoxifen-by-ER interaction. Tamoxifen ineffective even in ER+.
Interaction +0.009 (p=0.81). No trastuzumab-by-HER2 interaction; trastuzumab provides no PFS benefit even in HER2+ patients in this dataset.
Interaction +0.101 (p=0.41). No significant brca1-by-olaparib interaction (point estimate small positive, n=1277 BRCA1+).
Interaction -0.016 (p=0.90). No brca2-by-olaparib interaction.
Interaction +0.042 (p=0.28). No sacituzumab-by-HER2-low interaction.
Interaction +0.041 (p=0.21). Slight heterogeneity but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment assignment is approximately balanced across biomarker subgroups (no channeling), so observed treatment effects are not confounded by biomarker-driven indication.

analyses.code: Cross-tabulate each treatment vs each biomarker (er, pr, her2_pos, her2_low, brca1, brca2, pik3ca, postmenopausal); compare P[Tx=1 | bm=1] vs P[Tx=1 | bm=0].
analyses.result_summary: All treatment use rates differ by <=2 percentage points across biomarker strata (e.g., trastuzumab 20.9% in HER2+ vs 19.9% in HER2-; olaparib 9.4% in BRCA1+ vs 9.9% in BRCA1-). Hence treatments are NOT preferentially given to biomarker-positive patients - the dataset behaves as random assignment with respect to biomarkers, not as a real EHR with channeling.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within er_positive=1 patients, treatment_tamoxifen prolongs pfs_months.
Within er_positive=1 AND postmenopausal=1 patients, treatment_tamoxifen prolongs pfs_months.
Within her2_positive=1 patients, treatment_trastuzumab prolongs pfs_months.
Within brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib prolongs pfs_months.
Within triple-negative (er_positive=0, pr_positive=0, her2_positive=0) patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan prolongs pfs_months.
Within triple-negative patients, treatment_pembrolizumab prolongs pfs_months.

analyses.code: Welch t-test on pfs_months by treatment_tamoxifen restricted to er_positive=1
analyses.result_summary: Among ER+ (n=34886): mean 4.881 on tamoxifen (n=10535) vs 4.858 off; +0.023, p=0.45. No effect.
n=21081: tamoxifen on=4.854 vs off=4.845; +0.010, p=0.81. No effect.
Among HER2+ (n=9060): trastuzumab on=4.254 (n=1890) vs off=4.334; -0.079, p=0.15. Point estimate slightly negative; not significant.
Among BRCA-any (n=2499): olaparib on=4.933 (n=239) vs off=4.587; +0.346, p=0.041. Marginal benefit, but probable confounding (see iter 10).
Among TNBC (n=10496): sacituzumab on=4.228 (n=1084) vs off=4.314; -0.085, p=0.22. No benefit.
Among TNBC (n=10496): pembrolizumab on=4.366 (n=1578) vs off=4.294; +0.072, p=0.22. No benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is reduced in pik3ca_mutation=1 patients (negative interaction).
treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is greater in pr_positive=1 patients (positive interaction, secondary to ER+ correlation).
treatment_palbociclib effect is enhanced in her2_low=1 patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction -1.665 (p<1e-300). Palbociclib effect +1.679 in PIK3CA-wt vs +0.014 in PIK3CA-mut. PIK3CA mutation completely abolishes palbociclib benefit.
Interaction +1.030 (p<1e-300). Palbociclib effect +0.443 in PR- vs +1.473 in PR+. PR+ enhancement, likely largely explained by ER co-positivity.
Interaction +0.383 (p=3.4e-67). Palbociclib effect +0.960 in HER2-non-low vs +1.343 in HER2-low. Modest enhancement in HER2-low.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_palbociclib markedly prolongs pfs_months in the joint subgroup er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0, with little or no benefit outside it.

analyses.code: Stratify by [er_positive=1 & her2_positive=0 & pik3ca_mutation=0] (n=18681); compare palbociclib on/off in target vs non-target.
analyses.result_summary: TARGET (n=18681): palbo on=7.209 (n=6571) vs off=4.300 (n=12110); diff +2.908, p<1e-300. NON-TARGET (n=31319): palbo on=4.319 vs off=4.316; diff +0.003, p=0.91. Adjusted palbociclib coef inside target = +2.92 (p<1e-300); outside target = +0.0004 (p=0.86). Effect is essentially confined to this triple-positive-style subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all pairs of binary features, exhaustive search will reveal whether tamoxifen, trastuzumab, olaparib, sacituzumab_govitecan, or pembrolizumab has any 2-feature subgroup with a meaningful (>0.3 month) and significant (p<0.01) effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: For each of 5 non-palbociclib treatments, iterate over all 4-cell subgroups defined by every pair of 12 binary features (er_positive, pr_positive, her2_positive, her2_low, brca1_mutation, brca2_mutation, pik3ca_mutation, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, node_positive, postmenopausal, sex_female), require n>=200 with >=30 in each treatment arm, and report Welch t-test where |diff|>0.3 and p<0.01.
analyses.result_summary: Tamoxifen: 0 hits. Trastuzumab: 0 hits. Sacituzumab govitecan: 0 hits. Pembrolizumab: 1 hit (sex_female=1 & brca1_mutation=1, n=581: pem on=4.05 vs off=4.74, diff -0.68, p=0.010 - opposite direction, likely chance given multiplicity). Olaparib: 4 hits, of which only er_positive=0 & brca2_mutation=1 (n=379, ola_n=32) showed positive direction (+0.880, p=0.007). Other olaparib hits were negative directions and likely chance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within er_positive=0 AND (brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1) patients, treatment_olaparib prolongs pfs_months.

analyses.code: Welch t-test on pfs_months in BRCAany & ER- subgroup (n=750)
analyses.result_summary: Olaparib on=5.013 (n=55) vs off=4.184 (n=695); unadjusted diff +0.829, p=0.0012. Apparent benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The unadjusted olaparib benefit in the BRCAany & ER- subgroup is driven by confounding (treated patients are older), and disappears after adjusting for age and prognostic covariates.

analyses.code: Compare baseline characteristics of olaparib-treated vs untreated within BRCAany&ER- (n=750); fit OLS pfs_months ~ treatment_olaparib + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo within the subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: Olaparib-treated were 3.1 years older on average (68.1 vs 65.0). After adjusting for age (with its strong +0.176/yr effect) and other prognostic covariates, the olaparib coefficient collapses to -0.003 (p=0.92). The unadjusted +0.83 was entirely explained by age imbalance. A triple-interaction OLS (treatment_olaparib*brca_any*er_positive) confirmed all interaction terms null (all p>=0.25). Olaparib has no real PFS benefit in this dataset, even in the BRCA/ER-negative niche.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strong positive association between age_years and pfs_months is robust within strata of ecog_ps and stage_iv (i.e., not an artifact of sicker young patients).

analyses.code: Pearson r(age_years, pfs_months) within each ECOG and each stage_iv stratum.
analyses.result_summary: Within ECOG strata: r=0.742 (ecog 0), 0.741 (ecog 1), 0.726 (ecog 2). Within stage strata: r=0.739 (stage_iv=0), 0.717 (stage_iv=1). The +0.7 correlation is essentially unchanged after stratification, confirming age is an independent strong positive prognostic factor in this synthetic cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CAwt, treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months decreases monotonically across ki67_pct quartiles (largest in low-ki67, vanishing in high-ki67).

analyses.code: In ER+/HER2-/PIK3CAwt (n=18681), bin ki67_pct into quartiles; compare palbociclib on vs off PFS by quartile.
analyses.result_summary: Q1 ki67<=7.5%: palbo on=9.231 vs off=4.301, diff +4.930. Q2 7.6-12.2%: 9.236 vs 4.270, diff +4.966. Q3 12.3-19.6%: 6.002 vs 4.281, diff +1.721. Q4 >=19.7%: 4.300 vs 4.350, diff -0.050. Strict monotone decline; effect roughly disappears above ~20% ki67.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CAwt, the treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months declines linearly with ki67_pct (negative interaction coefficient).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ treatment_palbociclib*ki67_pct + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo, fit within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CAwt (n=18681).
analyses.result_summary: Main treatment_palbociclib coef +5.06; treatment_palbociclib:ki67_pct interaction -0.138/% (p<1e-300). Implied palbociclib effect: +4.37 at ki67=5, +3.68 at ki67=10, +2.99 at ki67=15, +2.30 at ki67=20, +1.61 at ki67=25, +0.92 at ki67=30. Clear continuous attenuation by ki67.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_palbociclib produces a very large pfs_months gain (~5 months) in the joint subgroup er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<=14, and a negligible effect outside it.

analyses.code: Define palbo_super = (er_positive=1)&(her2_positive=0)&(pik3ca_mutation=0)&(ki67_pct<=14). Compare palbociclib on vs off, both unadjusted and adjusted (age, ecog, stage_iv, brain_mets, albumin, weight_loss).
analyses.result_summary: palbo_super covers 21.7% of the cohort (n=10828). Inside super: palbo on=9.228 (n=3853) vs off=4.285 (n=6975); unadjusted diff +4.943 (p<1e-300); adjusted +4.977 (p<1e-300). Outside super (n=39172): unadjusted diff +0.007 (p=0.75) - essentially no effect. This is the best-supported treatment-effect subgroup definition for palbociclib in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusted univariate, biomarker-interaction, and exhaustive 2-way subgroup screens, no subgroup definition yields a significant adjusted positive PFS benefit for treatment_tamoxifen, treatment_trastuzumab, treatment_olaparib, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan, or treatment_pembrolizumab.

analyses.result_summary: Tamoxifen: null in all subgroups including ER+, ER+/postmenopausal, ER+/PR+, ER+/HER2-. Trastuzumab: null in HER2+, HER2+/ER+, HER2+/ER-; main effect slightly NEGATIVE (-0.06, p=0.029) which disappears after adjustment. Olaparib: only nominally significant subgroup was BRCAany&ER- which fully attenuates after adjusting for age imbalance. Sacituzumab govitecan: null in TNBC, HER2-low, HER2-low/HR+; the marginally positive multivariable coefficient (+0.033, p=0.05) is barely above noise. Pembrolizumab: null in TNBC, TNBC/PIK3CAwt, PIK3CAwt; small overall negative effect (-0.07, p=0.018) is not biologically meaningful (~2 days). None of these five treatments have a real PFS-extending subgroup in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The cohort has unusual demographic features for a labeled breast cancer dataset: only ~45% female, with PFS improving with age - both consistent with synthetic data rather than real EHR breast oncology.

analyses.code: Compute mean(sex_female), and compare PFS by sex; review age trend.
analyses.result_summary: sex_female mean = 0.447 (n_female=22371, n_male=27629). Mean PFS 4.686 in females vs 4.699 in males (p=0.56). Age decile means show monotone PFS rise from 1.70 (median age 47) to 7.84 (median age 82). Both observations are atypical for breast cancer EHR data and suggest the dataset is synthetic; the analysis is therefore best interpreted as exploring the data-generating process implemented by the dataset creator rather than as a real epidemiologic finding.
